commands.review_summary:
The branch extracts abort lock-release logging into a helper, calls it for manual and timeout embedded-run aborts, adds helper tests, and adjusts plugin prerelease test expectations for Docker stats sampling.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +48. Total +68 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main only calls releaseHeldLockForAbort inside the timeout-abort branch, so a manual abort skips the retained lock release. I did not run a live manual-stop retry flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer labeling, mock-only proof, a dirty current-main merge, and a proof-positive parallel PR make this a human canonical-landing decision rather than an automated repair lane.
